scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: the 5 legacy-parity read streams are unchanged (campaigns, segments, subscribers, templates, automations), plus a new organization_users read stream and 5 new write actions covering subscriber/segment upsert, campaign-recipient unsubscribe, individual message send, and automation-workflow triggering — the full documented mutation surface for Smaily's PHP API. Bulk contact-import/opt-in/forget/action-log/campaign-launch/campaign-statistics endpoints are excluded as out_of_scope (breadth-vs-cost triage: statistics/logs are not syncable object streams, and launch/import/opt-in/forget are lower-priority mutations than the 5 covered here) or requires_elevated_scope where documented as organization-admin-only.

excluded.reason: campaign create-and-launch is a full compound send-a-real-campaign action (recipient list selection, content, scheduling) beyond a single-record declarative write; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage for this pass
bulk opt-in confirmation action, narrower value than the already-covered create/update-subscriber write; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
irreversibly erases a subscriber's personal data account-wide (GDPR right-to-be-forgotten action), a materially higher-risk operation than this pass's covered mutations
message engagement-action log (sends/opens/clicks/opt-outs) explicitly time-windowed to the last 30 days by the API itself; an analytics/audit log, not a stable syncable object stream
subscriber engagement-action log, same shape/rationale as the message action log above

single-campaign statistics detail keyed by id; overlaps the campaigns list stream's own per-item shape for the object-identity fields, and the campaign-statistics-specific fields are an analytics rollup outside this pass's scope
